decisions.rationale: Create individual ledger accounts per member instead of relying solely on consolidated accounts. Enables per-member trial balance from the ledger alone and provides a proper audit trail at the account level.

decisions.decision: Shares + Savings on registration; Loan Receivable on demand
decisions.rationale: When a member is created, auto-create two sub-accounts: Member Shares (under parent 2001) and Member Savings (under parent 2002). Loan Receivable sub-account (under 1002) is created only when the member takes their first loan, avoiding unused accounts.
Sub-account codes use the parent account code as prefix and the member number as suffix. Human-readable, easy to trace, and leverages the existing unique member number.

Follows the existing event-driven pattern (16 events already in sacco-common). FinancialEventListener in sacco-ledger will handle MemberCreatedEvent to create the per-member sub-accounts. Decouples member module from ledger module.

decisions.decision: Keep Liquibase-only seeding for system accounts
decisions.rationale: Current 13 system accounts seeded via Liquibase migrations (002, 004, 005) are sufficient. No additional system accounts needed. New system accounts added as new changesets.

constraints: Account entity needs a parentAccountCode or parentId field to link sub-accounts to their parent
Account entity needs a memberId field (nullable) to associate per-member accounts
accountCode column length is 20 chars — format '2001-M001' fits within this
MemberCreatedEvent must carry memberId and memberNumber (needed for account code generation)
Existing FinancialEventListener handles 11 event types — MemberCreatedEvent listener follows same pattern
Loan sub-account creation must be triggered from LoanService when first loan is applied
members.shareBalance field must remain in sync with the per-member shares sub-account balance
openQuestions: Should existing members get retroactive sub-accounts via a one-time migration, or only new members going forward?
Should the per-member account balance be the source of truth, replacing members.shareBalance over time?
Should journal entries reference the per-member sub-account or continue using the consolidated parent account?
